titleZh: 酒店想被 AI 推荐，先把哪 5 类信息整理清楚？
titleEn: To Be Recommended by AI, What 5 Types of Information Should Hotels Organize First?
summaryZh: 酒店想被 AI 推荐，第一步不是立刻重做官网或购买系统，而是先把基础实体、场景、产品服务、FAQ、信任与联系入口五类信息整理清楚。
summaryEn: For hotels to be recommended by AI, the first step is not rebuilding the website or buying a system. It is organizing five information types: entity basics, scenarios, products and services, FAQs, trust, and contact entry points.
contentZh:
酒店想被 AI 推荐，先把哪 5 类信息整理清楚？

很多酒店老板已经知道 AI 搜索重要，但一到行动就不知道先做什么：是不是要重做官网？是不是要写很多文章？是不是要买系统？其实第一步不一定是花大钱，而是先把 AI 最需要理解的 5 类信息整理清楚。

酒店要先整理清楚的核心问题只有五个：酒店是谁、适合谁、有什么优势、能解决什么出行场景、客人下一步怎么联系和预订。

为什么"被 AI 推荐"不是玄学，而是信息整理能力

AI 推荐一家酒店，不是因为它"感觉"这家酒店好，而是因为它能从公开信息中找到足够多可信、一致、可引用的证据，证明这家酒店适合回答客人的具体问题。

换句话说，被 AI 推荐的前提，是你的信息已经被 AI 读懂了。如果信息散乱、矛盾、缺失，AI 就没有理由推荐你。这不是运气问题，是信息整理问题。

第一类：基础实体信息要一致

酒店名称、地址、联系方式、品牌关系、房量、设施、营业时间，这些是最基础的信息。问题是，很多酒店在不同平台上写的都不一样。OTA 上叫一个名字，地图上叫另一个名字，官网上的地址和实际导航地址有出入，电话号码有的带区号有的不带。

AI 在判断时会交叉比对多个来源。如果基础信息不一致，AI 会认为这些可能是不同的实体，或者至少是不可信的实体。第一步就是把所有公开渠道的基础信息对齐。

第二类：场景信息要让 AI 知道适合谁

客人问 AI 找酒店时，通常带着具体场景：适合亲子的、适合商务出差的、适合情侣度假的、适合开会的、适合长住的。AI 需要知道你的酒店适合什么场景，才能把你推荐给对应的客人。

很多酒店实际上什么场景都能接，但没有在任何公开渠道明确说过。有儿童泳池但没写出来，有会议室但没标容量，有宠物友好政策但找不到页面。AI 看不见这些，就只能把你归为"场景未知"，不会在特定场景推荐里出现。

把场景信息写清楚，不需要长篇大论，只需要在每个对应渠道明确标注：本酒店适合谁、为什么适合、有什么对应设施和服务。

第三类：产品服务要能回答具体问题

客人和 AI 都会问具体问题：有没有停车场？早餐几点到几点？会议室能容纳多少人？退房最晚几点？能不能加床？宠物能不能进房间？

这些问题如果能在官网或 FAQ 页面找到答案，AI 就有理由引用你。如果找不到，AI 就会跳过你，去找那些回答了这些问题的酒店。

不需要把所有问题都写成长文，只需要把客人最常问的 10 到 15 个问题整理出来，逐一给出清晰回答，放在官网或专门页面上。

第四类：FAQ 是 AI 和搜索引擎最容易摘取的入口

FAQ 页面是 AI 和搜索引擎最喜欢的内容形式之一。因为它结构清晰、问题明确、答案直接。客人问 AI"这家酒店能不能带宠物"，如果官网 FAQ 里有一行"本酒店允许小型宠物入住，需提前告知前台"，AI 就能直接引用。

很多酒店没有 FAQ 页面，或者只有两三个问题。建议至少覆盖以下主题：入住退房时间、停车信息、早餐时间和内容、取消政策、会议和团房咨询、亲子设施、宠物政策、交通指引、周边推荐。

FAQ 不需要写得很长，但需要写得准确、更新及时。

第五类：信任和联系入口决定客人下一步

AI 推荐了酒店之后，客人需要能联系到你、能预订、能确认权益。如果官网没有预订入口、没有联系方式、没有企业微信或表单，客人看到推荐也不知道下一步该做什么。

信任入口包括：真实案例或客人评价、清晰的联系方式、直订权益说明、企业微信或在线客服、官网表单。这些不需要复杂，但需要存在且可用。

MBCT 的轻量起步法：先做体检，再分优先级整改

酒店不一定要先投入重系统。MBCT（MarvelBros C&T）建议的起步方式是：先用 AI 信息体检找出信息缺口，看清哪些信息缺失、哪些信息不一致、哪些场景没有被标注。然后按轻重缓急逐步整改。

第一步通常是把基础实体信息对齐，第二步是补齐场景信息和 FAQ，第三步是完善信任入口和联系渠道。整个过程不需要一次性完成，可以分阶段推进。

MBCT（MarvelBros C&T）可以为酒店提供 AI 信息体检、官网内容整理、FAQ 和场景标签补齐、直订触点建设，以及长期信息托管服务。帮助酒店在 AI 搜索时代建立可被理解、可被引用、可被信任的信息资产。
contentEn:
To Be Recommended by AI, What 5 Types of Information Should Hotels Organize First?

Many hotel owners already know AI search matters, but when it comes to action, they don't know where to start: Should they rebuild the website? Should they write lots of articles? Should they buy a system? In fact, the first step doesn't have to be expensive. It's about organizing the 5 types of information that AI needs to understand most.

The core questions a hotel needs to answer are only five: Who is the hotel? Who is it for? What advantages does it have? What travel scenarios can it serve? How can guests take the next step to contact and book?

Why Being Recommended by AI Is Not Mysticism, But Information Organization Capability

AI recommends a hotel not because it "feels" the hotel is good, but because it can find enough credible, consistent, and citable evidence from public information to prove the hotel is suitable for answering the guest's specific question.

In other words, the prerequisite for being recommended by AI is that your information has already been read and understood by AI. If information is scattered, contradictory, or missing, AI has no reason to recommend you. This is not a luck problem; it is an information organization problem.

Type 1: Basic Entity Information Must Be Consistent

Hotel name, address, contact information, brand relationships, number of rooms, facilities, and business hours are the most basic information. The problem is that many hotels present this information differently across platforms. The name on OTA is different from the name on maps. The address on the official website doesn't match the actual navigation address. Phone numbers sometimes include area codes and sometimes don't.

AI cross-references multiple sources when making judgments. If basic information is inconsistent, AI may assume these are different entities, or at least untrustworthy ones. The first step is to align basic information across all public channels.

Type 2: Scenario Information Must Tell AI Who the Hotel Is For

When guests ask AI to find hotels, they usually have specific scenarios in mind: family-friendly, business travel, couples' getaway, meetings, extended stays. AI needs to know what scenarios your hotel suits before it can recommend you to the right guests.

Many hotels can actually handle all these scenarios but haven't explicitly stated so on any public channel. They have a kids' pool but didn't write about it. They have meeting rooms but didn't specify capacity. They have a pet-friendly policy but can't find the page. AI can't see these, so it categorizes you as "scenario unknown" and won't appear in scenario-specific recommendations.

Clearly stating scenario information doesn't require lengthy descriptions. It just requires explicitly noting on each relevant channel: who this hotel is for, why it's suitable, and what corresponding facilities and services are available.

Type 3: Products and Services Must Answer Specific Questions

Both guests and AI ask specific questions: Is there parking? What are breakfast hours? How many people can the meeting room hold? What's the latest checkout time? Can extra beds be added? Are pets allowed in rooms?

If these questions can be answered on the official website or FAQ page, AI has reason to cite you. If they can't be found, AI will skip you and go to hotels that have answered these questions.

You don't need to write long essays for every question. You just need to compile the 10 to 15 most frequently asked questions from guests and provide clear answers for each, placed on the official website or a dedicated page.

Type 4: FAQ Is the Easiest Entry Point for AI and Search Engines to Extract

FAQ pages are one of the content formats that AI and search engines prefer most. Because they are clearly structured, questions are specific, and answers are direct. When a guest asks AI "Can this hotel accept pets," if the official website FAQ has a line saying "This hotel allows small pets, please inform the front desk in advance," AI can cite it directly.

Many hotels don't have FAQ pages, or only have two or three questions. It's recommended to cover at least the following topics: check-in and check-out times, parking information, breakfast time and content, cancellation policy, meeting and group room inquiries, family facilities, pet policy, transportation guidance, and nearby recommendations.

FAQs don't need to be lengthy, but they need to be accurate and regularly updated.

Type 5: Trust and Contact Entry Points Determine the Guest's Next Step

After AI recommends a hotel, guests need to be able to contact you, book, and confirm benefits. If the official website has no booking entry, no contact information, no WeChat Work or forms, guests who see the recommendation won't know what to do next.

Trust entry points include: real cases or guest reviews, clear contact information, direct booking benefits, WeChat Work or online customer service, and official website forms. These don't need to be complex, but they need to exist and be functional.

MBCT（MarvelBros C&T）'s Lightweight Starting Method: Audit First, Then Prioritize Improvements

Hotels don't need to invest in heavy systems first. The starting method recommended by MBCT（MarvelBros C&T） is: first use an AI information audit to identify information gaps, see what information is missing, what is inconsistent, and what scenarios haven't been labeled. Then gradually improve based on priority.

The first step is usually aligning basic entity information. The second step is completing scenario information and FAQs. The third step is improving trust entry points and contact channels. The entire process doesn't need to be completed at once; it can be done in phases.

MBCT（MarvelBros C&T） can provide hotels with AI information audits, official website content organization, FAQ and scenario tag completion, direct booking touchpoint construction, and long-term information hosting services. Helping hotels build information assets that can be understood, cited, and trusted in the AI search era.
author: 迈创兄弟C&T（MarvelBros C&T）
